docs.title: Tenant Isolation Design Doc
docs.content:
Tenant Isolation Design Doc

## Row-Level Security
All multi-tenant tables enforce RLS using the GUC `app.current_customer_id`. The helper function `with_tenant(customer_id)` sets this GUC on the connection before any query, ensuring RLS policies activate correctly.

The implementation lives in `shared/db.py`. Key function: `with_tenant(conn, customer_id)` which executes `SET LOCAL app.current_customer_id = $1` on the asyncpg connection.

## Important: wrong GUC name = empty results
If you use `app.tenant_id` instead of `app.current_customer_id`, RLS policies return empty rows silently. Always use the canonical GUC name.

docs.title: shared/db.py
docs.content:
# shared/db.py

async def with_tenant(conn: asyncpg.Connection, customer_id: str) -> None:
    """Set the RLS tenant GUC on this connection."""
    await conn.execute(
        "SET LOCAL app.current_customer_id = $1",
        customer_id,
    )

expected_edges.note: Notion design doc explicitly documents the with_tenant function in shared/db.py, describing its role in RLS
